summary: This is an advice column where 'Aunt Pythia' (Cathy O'Neil) responds to reader questions on personal and social dilemmas. She advises a gay math student on how to address a friend's inappropriate sexual advances toward straight colleagues, suggesting direct but compassionate confrontation. She responds to a website navigation complaint with self-deprecating honesty about WordPress limitations. She counsels someone struggling with infidelity to have an honest conversation with their partner about their nature rather than making empty promises. Finally, she humorously deflects a question about math study recommendations by admitting she learns from Wikipedia and people, not textbooks.
key_points: Direct, honest confrontation is the best approach when a friend's behavior is harmful - even if it causes temporary shame and anger
When dealing with recurring infidelity, honesty about one's nature is preferable to empty promises of change
The 'six-month pass' concept suggests newly-out individuals deserve grace while adjusting, but not indefinitely
Self-deprecating humor can be an effective way to acknowledge limitations while still engaging with readers
Unwanted sexual advances are inappropriate regardless of the gender or orientation of those involved
money_quotes: Think of it as powerful self-love which has been donated to you by a good friend, along with a strong cup of tea and a delicious piece of chocolate babka from Breads Bakery.
It's that time again when I take your perfectly reasonable questions and utterly fuck them up with terrible suggestions.
My opinion (barring exceptional cases) is that people who come out before the age of 25 should get a 6-month pass to clean up whatever shit they brought with them from their straight days.
For the cheaters I know, at least the successful ones, they don't lie to their partners and pretend they'll never again stray.
For fuck's sake, don't wait until you are dying to fuck some cutie at work to bring up this topic, because that will come out and then jealousy will ensue.
I learned everything I know from wikipedia and other people. I basically never read technical books.
Congratulations, you've wasted yet another Saturday morning with Aunt Pythia!
key_insight: Honest, direct communication about difficult truths - whether confronting a friend's harmful behavior or acknowledging one's own nature to a partner - is more valuable than comfortable evasions or empty promises.
